title: ranunculus geminatus
intro: Ranunculus geminatus, also known as twin-flower buttercup, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white flowers with two petals that are larger than the other five. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus geminatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus geminatus can be made into a salve or ointment to apply topically, or used in teas and infusions to support overall health and well-being.
